howItWorks.intro: This trade war impact calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cost Increase = Product Cost × Import Content% × Tariff Rate%
Price Increase = Cost Increase × Pass-Through Rate%
Absorbed Cost = Cost Increase - Price Increase
Annual Impact = Absorbed Cost × Annual Units
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Who pays for tariffs — importers or consumers?
faq.answer: Initially, importers pay the tariff. Studies show that 60-100% of tariff costs eventually pass through to consumers as higher prices, depending on market competition and demand elasticity.

faq.question: How do tariffs affect profit margins?
faq.answer: If you can't fully pass costs to consumers, your margin shrinks. A 25% tariff on 60% imported content adds $7.50 to a $50 product. Absorbing even half means $3.75 less profit per unit.

faq.question: What is tariff pass-through rate?
faq.answer: Pass-through is the percentage of tariff costs passed to consumers via higher prices. Competitive markets with many alternatives see lower pass-through (30-50%). Unique products can pass through 80-100%.

faq.question: Is the Trade War Impact Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Trade War Impact Calculator?
sections.body: The Trade War Impact Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your product unit cost, imported content, new tariff rate, you get instant results including cost increase per unit, new selling price, margin impact. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cost increase per unit right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your product unit cost and need to find the right cost increase per unit. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Product Unit Cost ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your product unit cost — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: imported content, new tariff rate, current selling price, annual unit sales, price pass-through. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cost increase per unit, new selling price, margin impact. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your product unit cost before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both cost increase per unit and new selling price — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your price pass-through, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
